slides.title: Test shows 12% gain yields $24M
slides.strapline: This is a one-sentence so what tagline showing the quantified implication and why leaders should act now.
body: Evidence bullet one quantifies baseline productivity at 100 units and expected uplift at 12%. The resulting delta supports an estimated $24M annualized impact after applying a 0.8 realization factor.

Evidence bullet two links process cycle-time reduction from 9.5 days to 6.8 days under pilot conditions. This implies capacity release equivalent to 120 FTE without changing service-level commitments.

Callout insight 1: What most leaders miss is that adoption variance, not model quality, explains over half of value dispersion across business units. A ten-point adoption swing changes annual value by roughly $6M.

Callout insight 2: Counterargument says governance gates slow delivery and erase gains in year one. Rebuttal is that gated deployment reduced rework by 35%, preserving timeline certainty and net economics.

chart.source: Source: Internal pilot logs (Q4 FY2025, n=14 teams); assumptions include 80% realization and stable demand mix.
